title: 10 best herbal tinctures for lymph node swelling
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for lymph node swelling are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate swollen lymph nodes by stimulating the immune system and reducing inflammation. The best herbal tinctures for this condition include Echinacea purpurea, Echinacea angustifolia, Astragalus membranaceus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Taraxacum officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Silybum marianum, Sambucus nigra, Solidago virgaurea, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, lymph node swelling can lead to fatigue, pain, and impaired immune function, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being, ultimately affecting work, relationships, and overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with lymph node swelling because it contains compounds like triterpenoid saponins, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These bioactive molecules help reduce inflammation and edema in the lymph nodes, allowing them to return to their normal function of filtering the blood and removing toxins. Additionally, the tincture's antimicrobial effects can also help combat infections that may be contributing to lymph node swelling, promoting overall immune system health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains a variety of anti-inflammatory compounds, including phenolic acids and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with lymph node inflammation.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Kansas Coneflower tincture, such as kaempferol and quercetin, help neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, which can exacerbate lymph node swelling and promote healthy cell function.

Immune-modulating properties: Kansas Coneflower has been traditionally used to support immune function and modulate the immune response, which can help regulate inflammation and reduce swelling in lymph nodes.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, which in turn can alleviate swelling in lymph nodes.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides have anti-inflammatory properties and can help regulate immune cell activity, leading to reduced swelling and inflammation in lymph nodes.

Cichoric Acid: This phenolic compound exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the underlying causes of lymph node swelling and promote overall immune system balance.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, particularly echinacoside and echinulin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Echinacea angustifolia leaves are often used for their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and promote lymphatic drainage.
Stems: Echinacea angustifolia stems are sometimes utilized due to their moderate levels of alkylamides, which can contribute to the plant's overall anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall after the first frost when the plant is dormant for about 6-8 weeks.
Clean and dry the harvested roots thoroughly to prevent contamination and preserve their potency for up to a year.
Chop the dried echinacea angustifolia roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction and tincture production.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part roots to 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar occasionally to facilitate extraction and tincture formation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tinctures helps with lymph node swelling because of its adaptogenic properties, which allow it to stimulate the body's natural response to stress and promote overall wellness. The herb has been shown to increase lymphatic circulation and reduce inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating swollen lymph nodes caused by infections, allergies, or other conditions. Additionally, Siberian ginseng tinctures may help to boost the immune system, which can also contribute to a reduction in lymph node swelling.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Siberian Ginseng's adaptogenic properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are often linked to inflammation and swelling in lymph nodes, by promoting a state of balance and calmness in the body.

Immunomodulatory activity: The tincture has been shown to stimulate the immune system, increasing white blood cell production and enhancing natural killer cell activity, which can help combat infection and reduce swelling in lymph nodes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Siberian Ginseng contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, such as saponins and flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in lymph nodes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_constituents: Eleutherosides: These triterpene saponins help with lymph node swelling by modulating the immune system and reducing inflammation, thereby alleviating swelling and discomfort.

Phenolic acids (especially ferulic and sinapic acids): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce lymph node swelling by mitigating oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area.

Glycosides (including eleutheroside E): These compounds have been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which may help regulate the immune response and reduce swelling in lymph nodes by promoting balance in the body's immune functions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary source of bioactive compounds in Eleutherococcus senticosus, particularly eleutherosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
Stems: Stems of Eleutherococcus senticosus contain eleutherosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to alleviate lymph node swelling.
Barks: Barks of the plant are also used to extract eleutherosides, which may contribute to its potential benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting lymphatic health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of eleutherococcus senticosus roots in the fall after the first frost has killed the plant.
Clean the harvested roots by gently scrubbing them with a soft-bristled brush and rinsing with cold water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a single layer at 40c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Combine 500g of the dried roots with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place before straining and bottling.

Ginkgolide B: Ginkgolide B, a sesquiterpene lactone, helps by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a substance that can cause inflammation and swelling in the lymph nodes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate lymph node swelling.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the dried ginkgo biloba leaves in the ethanol for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids.
Press the cheesecloth to extract any remaining liquid and combine the liquid with water to achieve a 20% ethanol concentration.
Transfer the final tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
